Stanford enrollment full course waitlist add class policy

content.content:
- content: 'Enrollment-control records separate assignment scope from lifecycle state.


    Assignment fields identify an opaque control class, the student, academic

    term, course, section, record identifier, and assignment time. A record for

    another term or section has a different scope even when the course number

    is the same.


    Lifecycle fields describe whether the identified record is active, expired,

    already used, or revoked. Assignment alone does not establish lifecycle

    state, and lifecycle state alone does not identify assignment scope.


    A control class such as REG-17, REG-22, or REG-31 is an administrative code.

    This field guide does not define what any class changes in the enrollment

    process. In particular, neither a matching assignment nor an active status

    establishes whether the correct portal step is enrollment, waitlist

    placement, prerequisite handling, or another operation. Operational meaning

    must be obtained from the applicable official enrollment-control policy.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 1
  title: Enrollment Control Record Field Guide
  url: null

args.query: REG-17 enrollment control class CS106B full section waitlist policy Summer 2026

args.query: Stanford enrollment full course waitlist add class policy

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Enrollment Controls & Troubleshooting | Student Services

  - url: https://studentservices.stanford.edu/my-academics/enroll-classes/enrollment-troubleshooting

  - source: Stanford Student Services

  - domain: studentservices.stanford.edu


  ## Content


  ## Enrollment troubleshooting


  A failed enrollment attempt can have several causes. Students should check whether
  an enrollment hold appears in Navigate Enrollment or Axess, whether their assigned
  enrollment time has begun, whether the correct academic term is selected, and whether
  the class has reached its capacity. A class that is closed or full will not enroll
  the student automatically; some full classes provide a waitlist option.


  Course requisites can also block enrollment. Prerequisites must be completed before
  registration, corequisites must be taken at the same time, and antirequisites prevent
  enrollment when overlapping coursework has already been completed.


  ## Enrollment-control records


  Some course sections use registrar enrollment-control records. A record is assigned
  to a student for a defined term, course, and section and carries an administrative
  class code. The lifecycle of the exact record must also be current: expired, revoked,
  or previously used records cannot be applied.


  An active record does not have a universal effect. The operational effect depends
  on its control class under the policy table below. Students and staff should not
  infer the next portal step from the existence or active status of a record alone.


  For a full section with an available waitlist, apply this fixed processing rule:


  - When the applicable control class **satisfies the section-capacity control**,
  select **Enroll** for the requested section rather than joining the waitlist.

  - When the applicable control class **does not satisfy the section-capacity control**,
  join the waitlist.


  ### Summer 2026 control-class policy


  | Control class | Required assignment scope | Prerequisite effect | Section-capacity
  effect |

  |---|---|---|---|

  | REG-17 | Exact student, term, course, and section | None | Satisfies the section-capacity
  control when the exact record is active, unused, unexpired, and not revoked |

  | REG-22 | Student, term, and course | Satisfies an approved prerequisite review
  only | Does not satisfy the section-capacity control |

  | REG-31 | Exact student, term, course, and section | None | Does not satisfy the
  section-capacity control; provides waitlist priority only |


  Every condition in the selected row must be bound to the current case. Assignment
  scope and lifecycle are separate checks. A record for another student, term, course,
  or section does not apply, and a scope-matched record cannot be used when it is
  expired, revoked, or already used.


  If no current record satisfies the relevant capacity control, the available path
  for a full section is the waitlist.


  ## Waitlists


  When a class is full, a waitlist may be available. The first eligible student is
  enrolled when space opens. Holds, time conflicts, and maximum-unit limits can cause
  a waitlisted student to be bypassed. Waitlists are processed periodically and are
  usually canceled around the Final Study List deadline.

  '
